_readme: sound    → path to .mp3 or .aiff (relative paths resolve from this directory)
video_url→ YouTube or any URL — opened in default browser
messages → pool of strings; one is picked at random each time
probability → 0.0–1.0 chance the alert fires at all (great for noisy events)
enabled  → false to silence an event entirely
Drop .mp3/.aiff files in the sounds/ folder and point sound keys at them.

UserPromptSubmit.title: 🏀 New Prompt
messages: Prompt received. Steph at half court. Everyone already knows what happens next.
Context window loaded. 200,000 tokens on the shot clock. Let's go.
New possession. Curry dribbles off his own screen. The metaphor writes itself.
Ball in hand. Steph just read your entire codebase in 0.3 seconds. That's illegal.
Steph never gets tired. Your API bill might, though.
Prompt submitted. Curry calls his own play. Coach Kerr nods and steps back.
Another one. Steph Curry and Claude Code share one trait: they do not stop.

PreToolUse_Write.title: ✍️ Rewriting the Playbook
messages: STEP BACK — Steph is rewriting your file from 40 feet. {tool} incoming.
Zero hesitation. Zero undo history. That's criminal.
He's not asking for a code review. He never does. {tool}.
Logo three. No safety net. Just vibes and {tool}.
Steph just edited your code without asking. Who does that? Exactly.
Pull-up jumper from the file system. Clean.
Writing. Not a draft. Not a sketch. Curry doesn't do those.

PreToolUse_Bash.title: 💨 Shell Break
messages: Terminal open. Steph says 'trust me' and hits Enter.
Fast break into the shell. Nobody asked if this was safe.
Bash: the one tool that can nuke your node_modules and win a championship.
Coast to coast. No dribble limit in the terminal.
Your file system sees Steph coming and starts sweating.
rm -rf vibes. (Probably fine. Probably.)
Steph in transition. The shell prompt never stood a chance.

PreToolUse_Read.title: 📼 Watching Film
messages: Scouting your codebase. Curry watches more film than any human should.
Reading files. Steph has seen this defense before. He already knows.
Globbing through the repo like it's OKC in overtime.
Grep active. Steph Curry has never not found what he was looking for.
Every token spent scouting saves three in execution. Classic Curry IQ.

PreToolUse_Web.title: 🌐 Beyond the Arc
messages: Reaching outside the context window for reinforcements.
Web search: Steph pulls up Stack Overflow from behind the logo.
Real-time data. Curry never plays without a scouting report.
Stepping outside the paint. The internet is also a three-point line.
The one honest moment: even Steph Curry has to Google something.

PreToolUse_Other.title: 🎲 Audible
messages: Curry calls an audible. The playbook said {tool}. He's doing it anyway.
Off-script. Kerr confused. Play worked. {tool}.
This was not in the pre-game plan. Steph does not care: {tool}.
Improvisation. The rarest and most dangerous Curry move.

Notification_Permission.title: 🛑 HARD STOP
messages: Steph is holding the ball at the free throw line and staring at you. Say something.
Claude wants to do something you might regret. The ref is waiting.
Draymond is about to make a decision. Only you can stop this.
This is the play where Kerr draws it up and Claude goes off-script. Your call.
Flagrant foul incoming unless you weigh in. No timeouts left.

Notification.title: 📣 Tap the Floor
messages: Steph tapped the floor twice. That means he needs you.
Mid-possession update. Curry does not pause for nothing.
Something happened. Claude is making eye contact. This is rare.
FYI from half court. Filed under: worth knowing.

Stop.title: 🙏 Night Night
messages: Night night. Context window: closed. Cursor: still.
Final buzzer. Your code is probably better. Steph's stats: classified.
Curry walks off. 0 turnovers. 1 masterpiece. See you next prompt.
Session complete. Even Draymond looks impressed.
Tokens at rest. The play worked. It always works.
